# Copilot Instructions — vision-plate-nutrition

Purpose: give an AI coding agent the concise, project-specific knowledge needed to be productive in this repo.

- Quick start
  - Install dependencies: `npm install`
  - Start dev server: `npm run dev` (Vite + React)
  - Build for production: `npm run build` (or `npm run build:dev` for development-mode build)
  - Preview a built site: `npm run preview`
  - Lint: `npm run lint`

- High-level architecture
  - Vite + React + TypeScript single-page app (entry: `src/main.tsx`).
  - Route-based pages live under `src/pages` (e.g. [src/pages/Index.tsx](src/pages/Index.tsx)).
  - Reusable UI and primitives are in `src/components` with design-system pieces in `src/components/ui`.
  - Global application state is placed in `src/contexts` (`AuthContext`, `BrandingContext`, `LanguageContext`).
  - Platform helpers and integrations are in `src/lib` (e.g. [src/lib/firebase.ts](src/lib/firebase.ts)).
  - External service integrations (Supabase client & generated types) are under `src/integrations/supabase` ([src/integrations/supabase/client.ts](src/integrations/supabase/client.ts)).
  - Server-side functions used at runtime are in the `supabase/functions/` folder at the repo root.

- Important runtime/integration notes
  - The project uses a `@/` path alias: `@/*` -> `src/*` (see `tsconfig.json`).
  - Supabase is the backend and also stores runtime configuration used by frontend code (e.g. Firebase config is fetched via a Supabase Edge Function named `get-firebase-config` — see `src/lib/firebase.ts`).
  - The Supabase client is generated and includes a header comment: do not edit `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` directly.
  - Firebase Cloud Messaging: the app fetches FCM config from Supabase and registers `/firebase-messaging-sw.js` (service worker is in `public/`). Edits to messaging behavior usually require touching both `src/lib/firebase.ts` and the SW in `public/`.

- Conventions & patterns to follow
  - UI: follow the `src/components/ui` primitives (shadcn-style components + `class-variance-authority` usage). Prefer reusing existing primitives over creating ad-hoc markup.
  - Forms: `react-hook-form` is used across the app. For rich text, `@tiptap/react` and related extensions are used (see blog editors under `src/components/blog`).
  - Data fetching: `@tanstack/react-query` patterns are used for server data. Keep queries colocated with consuming components when feasible.
  - Auth/session: Supabase client persists sessions to `localStorage` (see `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`).
  - Generated files: treat `/src/integrations/supabase/*` and any `supabase`-generated artifacts as generated — regenerate rather than editing in place.

- Where to look for examples
  - Admin UI / analytics: `src/pages/AdminAnalytics.tsx` and `src/components/admin/*` show usage patterns for charts, date filters, and server calls.
  - Notifications: `src/lib/firebase.ts` shows how the app fetches config from Supabase, registers the SW, and obtains FCM tokens.
  - Routing + layout: `src/layout/MainLayout.tsx` and `src/layout/AdminLayout.tsx` show layout composition and route wrappers.

- Developer gotchas
  - Environment variables follow Vite conventions (e.g. `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`, `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`). Ensure `.env` values are present when running locally.
  - Some files are generated by tooling (Supabase types) — the project README indicates Lovable scaffolding; be careful when modifying generator outputs.
  - There are no automated tests in the repo; rely on the dev server and manual UI checks. Use `npm run lint` to check for lint problems.

- Changes & PR guidance for AI agents
  - Keep changes minimal and self-contained. Update or add unit-like checks only where existing patterns show such tests (none are present).
  - If editing runtime config flows (Firebase, Supabase functions), update both the client (`src/lib/...`) and the corresponding server function under `supabase/functions/`.
  - When adding new pages, follow existing page names and add routes via React Router patterns already present in `src/main.tsx` or the layout wrappers.

If any of these sections need more detail or you want me to include additional repo examples, tell me which area to expand.
